question_text: A 2 kg block is sliding across a rough surface with an initial velocity of 10 m/s. The friction force is 8 N. Calculate the acceleration of the block and determine whether it will eventually stop.
correct_answer: Acceleration = -4 m/s² (deceleration). Yes, the block will eventually stop because friction is causing a negative acceleration.
explanation: Using F = ma, the friction force of 8 N causes an acceleration of 8 ÷ 2 = -4 m/s². The negative acceleration means the block is slowing down. Eventually, its velocity will reach zero and it will stop.
